## Worth mentioning

### 1. GitLost: GitHub's AI agent tricked into leaking private repos

- Source: [noma.security](https://noma.security/blog/gitlost-how-we-tricked-githubs-ai-agent-into-leaking-private-repos/)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Security researchers tricked GitHub's AI agent via prompt injection into leaking private repository contents.

Builder/operator takeaway: Directly changes how a solo dev should scope tokens and permissions for coding agents with GitHub access.

Uncertainty: Unclear whether GitHub has fully remediated the specific exfiltration paths described.

### 2. Vercel acquires Better Auth to accelerate open source auth

- Source: [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/blog/vercel-acquires-better-auth)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: vendor_claim, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Vercel acquired Better Auth and its core team to continue the open-source auth library and build agent identity.

Builder/operator takeaway: Dependency-direction news for a very widely used OSS auth library; agent-identity roadmap is decision-relevant for agent builders.

Uncertainty: Long-term licensing/governance of Better Auth under Vercel is not yet demonstrated.

### 3. sqlite-utils 4.0: schema migrations, nested transactions, compound foreign keys

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/7/sqlite-utils-4/)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- sqlite-utils 4.0 shipped with database schema migrations, nested transactions, and compound foreign keys, plus breaking changes.

Builder/operator takeaway: Widely used SQLite tooling with breaking changes and a genuinely useful new migrations capability.

### 4. Chat SDK adds Dial support (SMS/MMS/iMessage + voice transcripts)

- Source: [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/changelog/chat-sdk-adds-dial-support)
- Category: Voice AI / Realtime Agents
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: vendor_claim, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Vercel Chat SDK added a vendor-official Dial adapter enabling bots to use SMS/MMS/iMessage and receive voice-call transcripts on real phone numbers.

Builder/operator takeaway: Phone-channel agent building block relevant to voice/calling products like CalenCall.

Uncertainty: Pricing and per-number provisioning constraints of Dial are not described in the changelog.

### 5. Give your eve agent GitHub tools

- Source: [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/changelog/github-tools-eve)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: vendor_claim, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- GitHub Tools released an eve integration that registers the full GitHub toolset with approval-gated write tools in minimal code.

Builder/operator takeaway: Agent tooling pattern (durable approval gates on write tools) applicable beyond this framework.

Uncertainty: Nine-lines claim untested; real-world approval UX unknown.

### 6. You shouldn't trust Trusted Publishing

- Source: [blog.yossarian.net](https://blog.yossarian.net/2026/07/07/You-shouldnt-trust-trusted-publishing)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Trusted Publishing eliminates long-lived credentials but provides no integrity guarantee for what is published, especially under CI compromise.

Builder/operator takeaway: Corrects a common false sense of security for anyone publishing packages via OIDC.

Uncertainty: Opinion/analysis piece; no new vulnerability disclosed.

### 7. OpenBSD through 7.9: local privilege escalation to root (CVE-2026-57589)

- Source: [nvd.nist.gov](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/cve-2026-57589)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- CVE-2026-57589 is a use-after-free in OpenBSD through 7.9 allowing local privilege escalation to root.

Builder/operator takeaway: Root privesc on a production OS; immediate action for affected systems.

Uncertainty: Patch/errata availability at time of triage not confirmed.

### 8. Tenda firmware ships hidden authentication backdoor

- Source: [kb.cert.org](https://kb.cert.org/vuls/id/213560)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-07-09T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- CERT/CC reports multiple Tenda firmware versions contain a hidden authentication backdoor.

Builder/operator takeaway: Actionable if any Tenda hardware is on your network; credible CERT sourcing.

Uncertainty: Full list of affected models/versions and vendor response not reviewed.
